response:
# Interactive Bar/Line Chart Accessibility Design Plan > **For Claude:** Use a11y-planner protocol. Review with a11y-critic after implementation. > **Compliance target:** WCAG 2.2 AA > **Users who need accessibility:** Screen reader users, keyboard-only users, low vision users, color vision deficient users, screen magnifier users, mobile users at narrow widths > **Assistive technologies:** NVDA, JAWS, VoiceOver, keyboard-only navigation **Feature:** An interactive SVG bar/line chart comparing 3-5 data series across 12 months, with legend, axes, tooltip, and data point hover/focus behavior. **Risk Level:** High **Component/Page Type:** Custom SVG data visualization with tooltip, legend, and table alternative --- ## Scope & Context This plan covers the accessibility layer for an existing React custom SVG chart renderer. The chart may render as either a bar chart or line chart, controlled by a prop. The visual design must remain unchanged, but accessibility structure, keyboard interaction, tooltip behavior, non-color indicators, and data access alternatives may be added. This plan commits to the following architecture: 1. Treat the SVG chart as a **single summarized image** for screen readers using `role="img"`, `aria-labelledby`, and `aria-describedby`. 2. Provide the complete dataset in an adjacent accessible HTML table. 3. Make visible chart data points keyboard focusable so keyboard users get the same tooltip details as pointer users. 4. Do **not** model the SVG itself as an ARIA grid. That would be fragile, verbose, and harder to maintain for 36-60 data points. The table is the primary structured screen reader data path; SVG focus is for tooltip parity and visual exploration. This is not claiming the SVG alone is a complete screen reader data interface. The complete non-visual data interface is the table. --- ## Semantic Structure Plan The chart component should render as a self-contained section with a heading, short summary, SVG visualization, keyboard-accessible data points, and a linked data table. The accessible name and description chain should make the relationship explicit: - Chart section heading names the visualization. - SVG gets a short data-oriented accessible name. - SVG description points to a concise summary and the data table. - The data table contains the full values by month and series. - Tooltip content is available on both hover and focus. ### Structure Diagram ```html

Monthly performance by series

Compares 3 to 5 data series across January through December. Full values are available in the data table after the chart.

Data table for monthly performance by series
Month Series A Series B Series C
January ... ... ...
``` ### Semantic Decisions - `section` with `aria-labelledby`: establishes the chart as a named page region. Satisfies WCAG 1.3.1 Info and Relationships and 2.4.6 Headings and Labels. - `h2`: provides navigable structure for screen reader users. Satisfies WCAG 1.3.1 and 2.4.6. - `svg role="img"`: communicates that the SVG is a single graphic, not a collection of individually exposed shapes. Satisfies WCAG 1.1.1 Non-text Content and 4.1.2 Name, Role, Value. - `aria-labelledby`: gives the SVG a meaningful accessible name. Satisfies WCAG 4.1.2. - `aria-describedby`: connects the SVG to the chart summary and data table caption. Satisfies WCAG 1.1.1, 1.3.1, and 4.1.2. - `table`, `caption`, `thead`, `tbody`, `th scope="col"`, `th scope="row"`: provide the complete structured data alternative. Satisfies WCAG 1.3.1 and 1.1.1. - Axis labels remain visually rendered in SVG and should also be represented in the summary/table caption, because SVG text alone is not the primary screen reader path. --- ## Interaction Pattern Design There is no perfect APG pattern for an exploratory chart. This plan uses established APG patterns where they apply and native semantics elsewhere. ### Interactive Elements Table | Widget | APG Pattern | Keyboard Model | ARIA Attributes | WCAG Citation | |---|---|---|---|---| | Chart SVG as whole | Image pattern / non-text content, using `role="img"` | Not tabbable as a whole unless needed for chart-level shortcut help | `role="img"`, `aria-labelledby`, `aria-describedby` | 1.1.1, 1.3.1, 4.1.2 | | Data point: bar or line node | APG Button pattern because it exposes a discrete interactive tooltip trigger | Tab moves between focusable points; Enter/Space toggles persistent tooltip; Escape dismisses tooltip | `role="button"` only if native SVG element cannot use native button semantics; `tabindex="0"`, `aria-describedby` when tooltip visible | 2.1.1, 2.4.3, 4.1.2, 1.4.13 | | Tooltip | APG Tooltip pattern: https://www.w3.org/WAI/ARIA/apg/patterns/tooltip/ | Appears on hover and focus; Escape dismisses; tooltip itself does not receive focus | `role="tooltip"`, trigger uses `aria-describedby` while tooltip is shown | 1.4.13, 4.1.2 | | Legend items | Static legend, not interactive unless series toggling exists | Not tabbable if not interactive | Text label plus swatch/pattern; decorative swatch `aria-hidden="true"` | 1.4.1, 1.3.1 | | Data table | Native HTML table | Native table navigation by screen reader; Tab only reaches interactive controls inside table if any | `caption`, `th scope="col"`, `th scope="row"` | 1.3.1, 1.1.1 | ### SVG Data Point Keyboard Model Each data point represents one month-series-value tuple. - `Tab`: moves to the next keyboard-focusable data point in DOM order. - `Shift+Tab`: moves to the previous keyboard-focusable data point. - `Enter`: shows or pins the tooltip for the focused data point. - `Space`: shows or pins the tooltip for the focused data point. - `Escape`: hides the tooltip and keeps focus on the current data point. - Arrow keys: optional enhancement only if implemented consistently. If used: - Right/Left moves by month within the same series. - Up/Down moves between series for the same month. - Focus remains on the new data point. - The focused visual mark receives the focus ring. For first implementation, Tab navigation is acceptable because there are 36-60 points. Arrow key navigation is recommended but not required if the data table is present and complete. Do not advertise arrow key support unless it is implemented. ### Data Point Accessible Names Each focusable data point must have a data-oriented name, not a visual description. Good accessible name: ```text Series A, March, 42 units ``` Bad accessible name: ```text Blue circle ``` For grouped bars, each bar receives its own name. For line charts, each node receives its own name. Decorative line segments between nodes should not be focusable and should be `aria-hidden="true"` if exposed. WCAG citations: 1.1.1 Non-text Content, 2.4.6 Headings and Labels, 4.1.2 Name, Role, Value. --- ## Focus Management Plan ### Tab Order Recommended order: 1. Skip link from page shell, if the dashboard has repeated navigation. 2. Chart section heading is reached through screen reader heading navigation, not Tab. 3. First focusable data point in the chart. 4. Remaining data points in month-major order: - January Series A - January Series B - January Series C - February Series A - February Series B - February Series C 5. Data table, if it contains interactive controls. If table is static, it should be read via screen reader table navigation, not Tab. 6. Next dashboard component. Month-major order is recommended because the X-axis is months and users compare series within each month. This matches the visual reading order and satisfies WCAG 2.4.3 Focus Order and 1.3.2 Meaningful Sequence. ### Focus Indicators Focused bars or nodes must show a visible focus indicator: - Minimum 3:1 contrast against adjacent colors. - Use a two-layer focus style where possible: dark outline plus light outer ring, or vice versa. - Focus ring must not be clipped by the SVG viewport. Add enough SVG padding or allow overflow where safe. - The focus indicator must be visible at 200% zoom and in high contrast mode. WCAG citations: 2.4.7 Focus Visible, 2.4.11 Focus Not Obscured, 2.4.13 Focus Appearance, 1.4.11 Non-text Contrast. ### Tooltip Focus Behavior When a data point receives focus: - Show the same tooltip that appears on hover. - Position it near the focused point if space allows. - If near container edges, flip or reposition the tooltip so it remains visible. - Do not move focus into the tooltip. - Add `aria-describedby` from the focused data point to the tooltip only while the tooltip is visible. - On blur, hide the tooltip unless it was pinned by Enter/Space. - On Escape, hide tooltip and keep focus on the data point. WCAG citations: 1.4.13 Content on Hover or Focus, 2.1.1 Keyboard, 2.4.3 Focus Order, 4.1.2 Name, Role, Value. ### Dynamic Content Insertion The tooltip is dynamic content, but focus should remain on the trigger data point. The tooltip should not steal focus. The screen reader path should rely on the data point accessible name and `aria-describedby`, not a live region for every hover/focus event. Do not create one live region per data point. That causes noisy repeated announcements and fragile screen reader behavior. --- ## State Communication Design ### State Communication Table | State | Visual Indicator | Programmatic Indicator | ARIA Attribute | WCAG Citation | |---|---|---|---|---| | Chart available | Visible SVG with heading and legend | Named graphic with summary and table reference | `role="img"`, `aria-labelledby`, `aria-describedby` | 1.1.1, 4.1.2 | | Data point idle | Normal bar/node style | Focusable element with accessible name | `tabindex="0"`, accessible name | 2.1.1, 4.1.2 | | Data point focused | Focus ring on bar/node; tooltip visible | Tooltip associated with focused point | `aria-describedby="chart-tooltip"` | 1.4.13, 4.1.2 | | Tooltip visible | Tooltip box with series, month, value | Tooltip role and trigger association | `role="tooltip"`, trigger `aria-describedby` | 1.4.13, 4.1.2 | | Tooltip hidden | Tooltip removed or visually hidden | No stale description on trigger | Remove `aria-describedby` or point only when relevant | 4.1.2 | | Series identity | Color swatch plus label and pattern/shape | Text label in legend and data point name | Swatch `aria-hidden="true"` if decorative | 1.4.1, 1.1.1 | | Loading, if present | Loading indicator and text | Status message and busy region | `aria-busy="true"`, `role="status"` | 4.1.3 | | Error, if present | Error text, icon, non-color cue | Programmatic alert/status | `role="alert"` for critical failure | 3.3.1, 4.1.3 | | Disabled, if future controls exist | Disabled styling plus text | Native `disabled` or `aria-disabled` for custom widgets | `disabled` or `aria-disabled="true"` | 4.1.2 | ### Tooltip Content Tooltip text should include: ```text Series A March Value: 42 units ``` If units are known, include them. If values are percentages, currency, counts, or rates, the accessible name and tooltip must include that unit. The tooltip must not rely on color alone to identify the series. --- ## Visual Accessibility Plan ### Color and Legend The legend currently uses colored swatches to identify 3-5 series. Color alone is not sufficient. Required plan: - Each legend item must include a visible text label. - Each series should also have a non-color visual encoding: - Bar chart: pattern fill, hatch, dot, stripe, or distinct border style. - Line chart: distinct line style and/or node shape, such as circle, square, triangle, diamond. - Tooltip must repeat the series name in text. - Data point accessible names must include the series name. WCAG citations: 1.4.1 Use of Color, 1.4.11 Non-text Contrast, 1.1.1 Non-text Content. ### Contrast - Axis labels, legend text, tooltip text: minimum 4.5:1 contrast. WCAG 1.4.3. - Large labels, if any: minimum 3:1 contrast. WCAG 1.4.3. - Bars, nodes, lines, focus rings, tooltip borders, and swatches: minimum 3:1 contrast against adjacent colors where they convey information. WCAG 1.4.11. - Tooltip background and foreground must meet 4.5:1 for normal text. WCAG 1.4.3. ### Reflow at 320px The chart is embedded in containers that may narrow to 320px. The component must not require horizontal scrolling to access information. Plan: - At narrow widths, preserve the visual chart responsively if it remains readable. - If axis labels or data point targets become too dense, show a simplified chart plus a visible “View data table” region immediately after the chart. - The data table must be available at 320px. It may use responsive table behavior, such as stacked rows by month, but it must not require two-dimensional horizontal scrolling to understand the data. - Do not hide the table on mobile. - Do not hide any series on mobile unless the table still contains all series and the chart summary clearly states the visual chart is simplified. WCAG citation: 1.4.10 Reflow. ### Target Size Focusable bars/nodes must have a keyboard focus target and pointer hit area of at least 24x24 CSS pixels where possible. For line nodes that are visually small, add an invisible SVG hit target around the node. - Visible node may remain small. - Hit target should be transparent but focusable. - Focus ring should appear around the visible data point or around the hit area in a way users can understand. WCAG citation: 2.5.8 Target Size Minimum. ### Motion If chart transitions or tooltip animations exist: - Respect `prefers-reduced-motion: reduce`. - Disable or reduce animated drawing, tooltip movement, and animated value transitions. - Do not use motion as the only way to indicate data changes. WCAG citations: 2.3.3 Animation from Interactions, 1.4.13 Content on Hover or Focus. --- ## Content Accessibility Plan ### Screen Reader Data Access The accessible HTML table is the primary full-data alternative. It should be rendered adjacent to the chart. Preferred visibility: - If space allows, make the table visible to all users under a “Data table” heading. - If product constraints require a visually hidden table, use a robust visually-hidden class that remains available to screen readers. - At 320px, the table should become available visibly or through an accessible disclosure. Do not use a hidden table with `display: none`, `visibility: hidden`, or `aria-hidden="true"`, because that removes it from assistive technology. ### Table Structure - Caption describes the chart and units. - First column is Month with `th scope="row"`. - Each series is a column with `th scope="col"`. - Values include units where needed. - Missing values should be text, such as “No data”, not blank cells. WCAG citations: 1.3.1 Info and Relationships, 1.1.1 Non-text Content. ### SVG Text and Decorative Elements - Axis labels are visible and should remain. - Decorative grid lines should be `aria-hidden="true"` or excluded from the accessibility tree. - Decorative swatches, line segments, and purely visual shapes should not be individually announced. - Data point focus targets are the only SVG children that should be keyboard focusable. WCAG citations: 1.1.1, 4.1.2. ### Link and Button Text If the component includes controls such as “View data table” or “Download CSV”: - Use descriptive text. - Do not rely on `title` for accessible names. - Icon-only buttons require `aria-label`. WCAG citations: 2.4.4 Link Purpose, 2.4.6 Headings and Labels, 4.1.2 Name, Role, Value. --- ## Testing Strategy ### Automated Testing Run axe-core against these states: - Bar chart default. - Line chart default. - Tooltip visible from hover. - Tooltip visible from keyboard focus. - Narrow 320px container. - Reduced motion enabled. - High contrast mode if test environment supports it. - Loading and error states if component supports them. Expected automated checks: - SVG has accessible name. - ARIA attributes are valid. - Table has valid caption and headers. - Contrast issues are detected where possible. - No focusable element is hidden from assistive technology. - No `aria-hidden` ancestor contains focusable data points. Axe will not prove tooltip announcement quality, keyboard order quality, or screen reader table usability. Those require manual testing. ### DOM Verification Tests must verify rendered DOM, not just React props: - SVG root has `role="img"`. - SVG root has `aria-labelledby` pointing to the chart title. - SVG root has `aria-describedby` pointing to summary and table caption. - Decorative SVG elements are not focusable. - Data points have accessible names. - Focusable data points have `tabindex="0"`. - Tooltip has `role="tooltip"`. - Focused data point receives `aria-describedby` when tooltip is visible. - Data table renders valid `caption`, `th scope="col"`, and `th scope="row"`. ### Keyboard Testing Test cases: - Tab reaches the first data point. - Tab proceeds through data points in month-major order. - Shift+Tab reverses through data points. - Focus ring is visible on each focused bar/node. - Focus shows the tooltip. - Enter or Space shows or pins tooltip. - Escape dismisses tooltip without moving focus. - Tooltip content is equivalent for hover and focus. - No keyboard trap exists inside the chart. - After the last data point, Tab moves to the next logical focus target. ### Screen Reader Testing Test with NVDA, JAWS, and VoiceOver. Scenarios: - Navigate by heading to the chart title. - Read the chart SVG and confirm it is announced as a named graphic with useful summary. - Confirm screen reader can reach and read the data table. - Navigate the table by rows and columns. - Focus a data point and confirm name includes series, month, value, and unit. - Trigger tooltip and confirm the extra description is available. - Confirm decorative grid lines, swatches, and line segments are not announced as noise. ### Visual and Responsive Testing - Verify text contrast at 4.5:1. - Verify non-text chart marks and focus rings at 3:1. - Verify legend is understandable without color. - Verify chart and table at 320px width. - Verify 200% zoom without horizontal page scrolling. - Verify reduced motion removes or minimizes animation. - Verify tooltip remains visible and dismissible at viewport edges. --- ## Implementation Tasks ### Task 1: Chart Semantic Wrapper 🔍 **Review checkpoint after this task** **Files:** - Chart component file - Chart accessibility helper, if used - Chart tests **Structure Stub:** ```html

[Chart title]

[Short chart summary]

``` **ARIA Attributes:** - `role="img"` on SVG: exposes chart as one graphic. WCAG 1.1.1, 4.1.2. - `aria-labelledby`: names the chart from visible heading. WCAG 4.1.2. - `aria-describedby`: links summary and table caption. WCAG 1.3.1, 4.1.2. **Tests:** - SVG has accessible name. - SVG description references existing elements. - Heading and summary are present. - Decorative SVG elements are not announced individually. **WCAG Criteria:** - 1.1.1 Non-text Content - 1.3.1 Info and Relationships - 4.1.2 Name, Role, Value --- ### Task 2: Accessible Data Table 🔍 **Review checkpoint after this task** **Structure Stub:** ```html
Data table for [chart title], values by month and series
Month [Series name]
January [value with unit]
``` **ARIA Attributes:** - Prefer native table semantics over ARIA roles. - Do not use `role="presentation"` on the table. - Do not hide table with `aria-hidden`. **Tests:** - Caption exists and is linked by SVG description. - Column headers use `scope="col"`. - Month cells use `scope="row"`. - All series and all 12 months are present. - Missing values are represented as meaningful text. **WCAG Criteria:** - 1.1.1 Non-text Content - 1.3.1 Info and Relationships - 1.3.2 Meaningful Sequence --- ### Task 3: Keyboard-Focusable Data Points and Tooltip 🔍 **Review checkpoint after this task** **Structure Stub:** ```html
[Series], [Month], [Value] [Unit]
``` **ARIA Attributes:** - `tabindex="0"` on interactive data point or its hit target: keyboard access. WCAG 2.1.1. - Accessible name on each data point: series, month, value, unit. WCAG 1.1.1, 4.1.2. - `role="tooltip"` on tooltip. WCAG 4.1.2. - `aria-describedby` from focused data point to tooltip while visible. WCAG 1.4.13, 4.1.2. **Keyboard Interactions:** - Tab: move through data points in month-major order. - Shift+Tab: reverse order. - Enter/Space: show or pin tooltip. - Escape: dismiss tooltip. - Optional arrows: only if fully implemented and tested. **Tests:** - Focus shows tooltip. - Hover shows same tooltip content. - Escape hides tooltip. - Focus remains on data point after Escape. - Data point names are meaningful. - Decorative line segments and gridlines are not focusable. **WCAG Criteria:** - 2.1.1 Keyboard - 2.4.3 Focus Order - 2.4.7 Focus Visible - 1.4.13 Content on Hover or Focus - 4.1.2 Name, Role, Value --- ### Task 4: Legend Non-Color Encoding 🔍 **Review checkpoint after this task** **Requirements:** - Keep existing colors. - Add visible series names. - Add pattern, shape, or line-style distinction per series. - Ensure tooltip and data point names include series names. **Tests:** - Legend is understandable in grayscale. - Each series can be identified without color. - Swatches are decorative if the text label provides the meaning. - Data point tooltip includes series name. **WCAG Criteria:** - 1.4.1 Use of Color - 1.4.11 Non-text Contrast - 1.1.1 Non-text Content --- ### Task 5: Responsive and Zoom Behavior 🔍 **Review checkpoint after this task** **Requirements:** - Chart container adapts to 320px width. - Data table remains available at 320px. - No horizontal page scroll is required to understand the data. - Focus rings are not clipped. - Tooltip repositions at edges. - Reduced motion preference is respected. **Tests:** - 320px viewport or container test. - 200% zoom test. - Keyboard navigation at narrow width. - Tooltip visibility at chart edges. - Reduced motion test. **WCAG Criteria:** - 1.4.10 Reflow - 1.4.4 Resize Text - 2.4.11 Focus Not Obscured - 2.3.3 Animation from Interactions --- ## a11y-Critic Review Checkpoints | Checkpoint | After Task | Focus Areas | |---|---|---| | 1 | Chart Semantic Wrapper | SVG name/description, summary quality, decorative SVG noise | | 2 | Data Table | Complete data alternative, table headers, caption, no hidden/inert table mistake | | 3 | Data Points and Tooltip | Keyboard access, tooltip parity, focus order, Escape behavior, accessible names | | 4 | Legend Encoding | Non-color alternatives, contrast, series identification | | 5 | Responsive Behavior | 320px reflow, 200% zoom, focus visibility, tooltip positioning | --- ## Architecture Overview The component uses a hybrid accessibility architecture: - SVG is exposed as a named graphic with a concise summary. - Full data is exposed through a native HTML table. - Data points remain keyboard focusable for tooltip parity and visual exploration. - Tooltip follows APG tooltip expectations and appears on both hover and focus. - Legend uses color plus non-color encodings. This avoids overloading the SVG with a complex ARIA grid model while still giving keyboard users access to the interactive tooltip behavior. --- ## Failure Modes This Plan Prevents - Hover-only tooltip that fails WCAG 1.4.13. - Legend relying on color alone, failing WCAG 1.4.1. - SVG announced as unlabeled graphic or noisy collection of paths. - Screen reader users receiving only a vague chart description instead of full data. - Keyboard users unable to inspect data points. - Focus rings clipped by SVG viewport. - Data table present visually but hidden from assistive technology. - Mobile layout requiring horizontal scrolling to understand chart data. - Decorative SVG gridlines and paths polluting the accessibility tree. --- ## References - WAI-ARIA APG Tooltip Pattern: https://www.w3.org/WAI/ARIA/apg/patterns/tooltip/ - WAI-ARIA Authoring Practices Guide: https://www.w3.org/WAI/ARIA/apg/ - WCAG 2.2 Quick Reference: https://www.w3.org/WAI/WCAG22/quickref/
